operating_state: Net sales increased 8% to $269.9 billion driven by 6% comparable sales growth, 24 net new warehouse openings to 914 locations globally, and membership growth to 81 million paid members. Executive membership penetration reached 73.6% of worldwide net sales while e-commerce grew 15% quarterly representing 7% of total sales. Core merchandise categories increased driven by fresh foods performance while warehouse ancillary businesses grew with gasoline operations through 747 stations.
cost_structure: Gross margin percentage increased 20 basis points to 11.12% driven by core merchandise improvements of 19 basis points, primarily fresh foods and co-branded credit programs, partially offset by LIFO charge from higher merchandise costs. SG&A expenses increased 11 basis points to 9.25% reflecting minimum wage increases to $20.00 and technology infrastructure investments. Kirkland Signature private-label expansion continued generating higher margins while maintaining competitive pricing strategies.
material_events: Board approved 12% quarterly dividend increase to $1.30 per share and repurchased 943,000 shares for $903 million under $4 billion authorization expiring January 2027. Gary Millerchip joined as CFO from Kroger with multiple executive transitions including Russ Miller retiring February 2026. Membership fees increased in U.S. and Canada effective September 2024, contributing to membership income growth.
macro_exposures: Foreign currency translation negatively impacted net sales by $1.9 billion and net income by $97 million with forward contracts hedging international inventory purchases. Gasoline price deflation reduced net sales while trade-related tariff actions affected merchandise costs dependent on goods type and policy timing. OECD Pillar 2 global minimum tax implementation had no material fiscal 2025 impact.
forward_outlook: Management plans up to 35 new warehouse openings including five relocations in fiscal 2026 with capital expenditures guided at $6.0-6.5 billion financed through operating cash flow. Technology investments will continue emphasizing digital capabilities and cybersecurity infrastructure while Kirkland Signature expansion is expected to increase sales penetration and margin contribution. Stock compensation vesting schedules changed from five-year to three-year terms for future grants.
uncertainty_profile: Cybersecurity threats increasing in frequency with potential operational disruption and regulatory penalties under evolving privacy regulations while third-party provider dependencies persist. Multiple legal proceedings include California labor class actions, opioid litigation, and federal investigations regarding prescription medications and EPA violations. Extended producer responsibility laws across states require new compliance processes and fee payments while commodity price fluctuations continue through variable-priced contracts.
